<objective>
Create the fiber detection engine and source map resolution modules that form the core of Phase 4.

Purpose: These modules extract React component data from DOM elements via main-world script injection and resolve original source file locations via source map parsing. They are self-contained building blocks wired together in Plan 02.

Output: Three new modules (fiber-main-world.ts, fiber-bridge.ts, source-resolver.ts), updated element-detector with CSS selector utility, WXT config with web_accessible_resources, schema with parentChain field, source-map-js installed.
</objective>

  <name>Task 1: Create main-world fiber detection script and supporting infrastructure</name>
  <files>

  </read_first>
  <action>
**1. Create `packages/extension/entrypoints/fiber-main-world.ts`** — WXT unlisted script using `defineUnlistedScript()`:

The script runs in the page's main world (has access to React internals). It communicates with the content script via CustomEvent on `document.currentScript`.

**Event listener setup:**
- Listen for `inspatch-fiber-query` CustomEvent on `document.currentScript`
- Event detail contains `{ selector: string }` — a CSS selector identifying the target element
- On receiving query, call `extractFiberData(selector)` and dispatch `inspatch-fiber-result` CustomEvent back on the same script element with the result as detail

**`extractFiberData(selector: string)` function:**
- `document.querySelector(selector)` to find the element
- If not found, return `{ componentName: null, parentChain: [], debugSource: null }`
- Find React fiber key by iterating `Object.keys(el)` for key matching `__reactFiber$` or `__reactInternalInstance$` prefix (per D-01)
- If no fiber key on the element, walk UP the DOM tree checking `el.parentElement` ancestors until finding one with a fiber key (handles text nodes, fragments — per Pitfall 5 from research)
- If still no fiber key found after reaching document root, return null result
- Call `walkFiberTree((el as any)[fiberKey])` with the found fiber

**`walkFiberTree(startFiber)` function:**
- Initialize: `componentName: string | null = null`, `parentChain: string[] = []`, `debugSource: { fileName: string; lineNumber: number } | null = null`
- Walk `fiber = fiber.return` upward until `fiber` is null
- At each fiber, call `getComponentName(fiber)` — if it returns a name:
  - If `componentName` is still null (first component found), set it as `componentName`
  - Also capture `fiber._debugSource ?? null` as `debugSource` (React 17/18 only — null in React 19 per research critical finding)
  - Push the name onto `parentChain`
- Return `{ componentName, parentChain, debugSource }`

**`getComponentName(fiber)` function:**
- If `fiber.type` is falsy, return null
- If `typeof fiber.type === 'string'`, return null (host element like div/span)
- If `typeof fiber.type === 'function'`, return `fiber.type.displayName || fiber.type.name || null`
- If `fiber.type.$$typeof` exists (wrapped component):
  - Check `fiber.type.$$typeof.toString()` — if contains `'forward_ref'`: return `fiber.type.displayName || fiber.type.render?.displayName || fiber.type.render?.name || null`
  - If contains `'memo'`: return `fiber.type.displayName || getComponentName({ type: fiber.type.type }) || null`
- Return null for unrecognized types

**2. Add `getUniqueSelector` to `packages/extension/entrypoints/content/element-detector.ts`:**

Export a new function `getUniqueSelector(el: Element): string` that generates a CSS selector usable with `document.querySelector()` in the main world:
- If `el.id` is truthy, return `#${CSS.escape(el.id)}`
- Otherwise, build a path walking from `el` up to `document.documentElement`:
  - For each element: `tagName.toLowerCase()`
  - If element has an id: prepend `#${CSS.escape(id)}` and stop walking
  - If multiple siblings share the same tagName: append `:nth-of-type(index)` (1-based)
  - Join with ` > ` separator
- This produces selectors like `#app > div > section:nth-of-type(2) > button`

**3. Update `packages/extension/wxt.config.ts`** manifest to add `web_accessible_resources`:

Add to the `manifest` object:
```
web_accessible_resources: [
  {
    resources: ['fiber-main-world.js'],
    matches: ['http://localhost:*/*'],
  },
],
```

This is REQUIRED — WXT does NOT auto-add unlisted scripts to web_accessible_resources (per WXT GitHub issue #536 and research Pitfall 3).

**4. Update `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`:**

Add `parentChain: z.array(z.string()).optional()` and `sourceColumn: z.number().optional()` to `ElementSelectionSchema` (after the existing `sourceLine` field).

Add the same `parentChain` field to `ChangeRequestSchema`.

Export the updated `ElementSelection` type (already auto-derived via `z.infer`).
  </action>

  <action>
**1. Install source-map-js dependency:**

Run `cd packages/extension && bun add source-map-js` to install version 1.2.1 (per D-07, project CLAUDE.md). Verify it appears in package.json dependencies.

**2. Create `packages/extension/entrypoints/content/source-resolver.ts`:**

This module runs in the content script context (same origin as the page, can fetch from localhost dev server).

**Exported types:**
```typescript
export interface SourceLocation {
  source: string;
  line: number;
  column: number;
}
```

**`resolveSourceLocation(scriptUrl: string, line: number, column: number): Promise<SourceLocation | null>`:**
- Call `getOrFetchSourceMap(scriptUrl)` to get a cached or fresh SourceMapConsumer
- If no consumer, return null
- Call `consumer.originalPositionFor({ line, column })`
- If `pos.source` is null, return null
- Normalize the source path: strip `webpack://` prefix, strip leading `./`, convert to project-relative path
- Return `{ source: pos.source, line: pos.line!, column: pos.column! }`

**`findComponentSource(componentName: string): Promise<SourceLocation | null>`:**
- This is the React 19 fallback when `_debugSource` is unavailable (per D-06 promoted to primary)
- Enumerate `document.scripts` — collect script elements with `src` attributes pointing to localhost
- For each script URL:
  - Call `getOrFetchSourceMap(scriptUrl)` to get the consumer
  - If no consumer, skip
  - Check `(consumer as any).sources` array for entries whose filename portion contains the component name (e.g., `componentName = "UserProfile"` matches source `src/components/UserProfile.tsx`)
  - Match logic: extract basename from each source path, check if it starts with `componentName` followed by `.tsx`, `.jsx`, `.ts`, or `.js`
  - If a matching source is found, search `(consumer as any).sourcesContent` at the same index for a line containing `function ${componentName}` or `const ${componentName}` or `class ${componentName}` or `export default function ${componentName}` or `export function ${componentName}`
  - If found, return `{ source: matchingSource, line: lineNumber, column: 0 }` where lineNumber is the 1-based line index of the match
  - If `sourcesContent` is unavailable or no definition line found, return `{ source: matchingSource, line: 1, column: 0 }` (file-level resolution)
- If no scripts match, return null

**`getOrFetchSourceMap(url: string): Promise<SourceMapConsumer | null>` (internal, not exported):**
- Maintain module-level `Map<string, SourceMapConsumer>` cache
- If URL is in cache, return cached consumer
- **Security:** Validate that URL starts with `http://localhost:` or `http://127.0.0.1:` before fetching (per research security domain — never fetch from non-localhost)
- Fetch the script URL text content via `fetch(url)`
- Try inline base64 source map first: regex `\/\/[#@]\s*sourceMappingURL=data:application\/json;(?:charset=utf-8;)?base64,(.+)$` with `m` flag
- If inline match: `JSON.parse(atob(match[1]))` to get raw map
- Try external .map file: regex `\/\/[#@]\s*sourceMappingURL=(.+\.map.*)$` with `m` flag
- If external match: resolve URL with `new URL(match[1].trim(), url).href`, fetch the .map URL, parse JSON
- If neither found, cache null for this URL and return null
- Create `new SourceMapConsumer(rawMap)`, cache it, return it
- Wrap all fetch/parse in try/catch — return null on any error (graceful degradation)

**`clearSourceMapCache(): void` (exported):**
- Clear the internal cache map — useful for page navigation/reload

**3. Create `packages/extension/entrypoints/content/fiber-bridge.ts`:**

This module manages main-world script injection and cross-world communication.

**Exported types:**
```typescript
export interface FiberResult {
  componentName: string | null;
  parentChain: string[];
  debugSource: { fileName: string; lineNumber: number } | null;
}
```

**Module state:**
- `let injectedScript: HTMLScriptElement | null = null`
- `let bridgeReady = false`
- `const pendingQueries = new Map<string, { resolve: (result: FiberResult) => void; timer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> }>()`

**`initFiberBridge(): Promise<void>`:**
- Import `injectScript` from `wxt/utils/inject-script` (per research — more robust than raw `chrome.scripting.executeScript`, per D-02 adjusted)
- Call `const { script } = await injectScript('/fiber-main-world.js', { keepInDom: true })`
- `keepInDom: true` ensures the script element stays in DOM for ongoing CustomEvent communication
- Set up result listener on the script element: `script.addEventListener('inspatch-fiber-result', handler)` where handler extracts `event.detail` as `FiberResult` and resolves the matching pending query
- Store `injectedScript = script` and `bridgeReady = true`
- Wrap in try/catch — if injection fails (e.g., page CSP blocks it), log warning and leave `bridgeReady = false`

**`queryFiber(selector: string): Promise<FiberResult>`:**
- If `!bridgeReady || !injectedScript`, return immediately with `{ componentName: null, parentChain: [], debugSource: null }`
- Generate a unique query ID (use simple incrementing counter)
- Create a Promise that:
  - Stores resolve callback in `pendingQueries` map keyed by query ID
  - Sets a 2-second timeout that resolves with null result and removes from map (fiber queries should be near-instant)
  - Dispatches `inspatch-fiber-query` CustomEvent on `injectedScript` with detail `{ selector, queryId }`
- The result listener (from initFiberBridge) matches `queryId` in the response detail, resolves the promise, clears the timeout, and removes from pending map
- Update `fiber-main-world.ts` to echo back `queryId` in the result event

**`isBridgeReady(): boolean`:**
- Return `bridgeReady` — lets consumers check if fiber detection is available
  </action>

  </verify>

  <done>
    source-map-js installed in extension package. Source resolver handles both inline base64 (Vite) and external .map (Next.js/CRA) source maps with in-memory caching and localhost-only URL validation. findComponentSource provides React 19 fallback via component name to source file heuristic matching. Fiber bridge injects main-world script via WXT injectScript with CustomEvent-based bidirectional communication, 2-second query timeout, and graceful degradation when injection fails.
  </done>
</task>

</tasks>

<threat_model>
## Trust Boundaries

| Boundary | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Main world → Content script | Fiber data from page context is fully untrusted — any page script could theoretically forge CustomEvents |
| Content script → Dev server | Source map fetches go to localhost dev server — must validate URLs to prevent SSRF to non-local origins |

## STRIDE Threat Register

| Threat ID | Category | Component | Disposition | Mitigation Plan |
|-----------|----------|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| T-04-01 | Tampering | fiber-main-world.ts CustomEvent | mitigate | Use unique event names (inspatch-fiber-*) dispatched on the injected script element specifically (not window) — page scripts cannot dispatch events on an element they don't have a reference to |
| T-04-02 | Tampering | fiber-bridge.ts | mitigate | Validate shape of received FiberResult data before trusting — check componentName is string or null, parentChain is string array, debugSource has expected shape |
| T-04-03 | Information Disclosure | source-resolver.ts URL fetch | mitigate | Only fetch URLs starting with http://localhost: or http://127.0.0.1: — reject all other origins before fetch |
| T-04-04 | Information Disclosure | source map paths | mitigate | Normalize displayed paths to project-relative format — strip webpack:// protocol, absolute filesystem prefixes before passing to sidebar |
| T-04-05 | Denial of Service | source-resolver.ts cache | accept | Large source maps (5MB+) could slow parsing. Acceptable for local dev tool — source maps are cached per script URL, limiting repeated parsing. No PII at risk. |
</threat_model>

8. No new linter errors introduced
</verification>
